q1.prompt: A 50-year-old farmer presents with persistent bloody diarrhea and abdominal pain; stool microscopy reveals large, ovoid trophozoites covered in cilia. Which morphological feature is most diagnostic for the causative ciliate?

options.label: A terminal spine on the cyst wall

q2.prompt: A public health official is investigating an outbreak of diarrheal disease in a community where the water supply is treated only with standard chlorination. Which characteristic of Giardia lamblia and Entamoeba histolytica explains why this treatment is insufficient?

options.label: Ability to replicate in the external environment
Resistance of the cyst stage to chlorine
Requirement for an intermediate snail host

q3.prompt: A 28-year-old immigrant from Sudan presents with intense generalized pruritus and blurred vision; physical examination reveals thickened skin and scattered maculopapular lesions. Which diagnostic procedure is required to confirm the presence of the suspected parasite?

q4.prompt: A patient develops a localized itchy rash ("swimmer's itch") after bathing in a freshwater lake; several weeks later, they present with fever and malaise, eventually progressing to portal hypertension. Which process is responsible for the chronic systemic manifestations of this disease?

options.label: Direct invasion of the brain parenchyma by cercariae
Immune reaction against eggs deposited in the tissues
Mechanical obstruction of the small intestine by adult worms
Migration of rhabditiform larvae through the lungs
Multiplication of trophozoites in the biliary tract

q5.prompt: During a routine stool examination of an asymptomatic food handler, spherical cysts measuring 14 µm in diameter are identified. The presence of four nuclei within these cysts is diagnostic for which organism?

q6.prompt: A 35-year-old man presents with fever, hemoptysis, and dyspnea after frequently consuming raw freshwater crabs. Which infective stage is responsible for this condition?

q7.prompt: A 36-year-old woman presents with recurrent right upper quadrant pain and fever; imaging and laboratory tests confirm the presence of recurrent pyogenic cholangiohepatitis. Which combination of pathogens is most likely associated with this clinical presentation?

q8.prompt: A physician is ordering a thick blood film for a patient with suspected lymphatic filariasis caused by Wuchereria bancrofti. At what time should the blood specimen be collected to maximize diagnostic sensitivity?

options.label: During the onset of a high fever
Mid-day when the patient is most active
Late afternoon during the peak of symptoms
At night due to the periodicity of the microfilariae

q9.prompt: A patient in a region endemic for lymphatic filariasis presents with significant lymphedema of the scrotum and recurrent "filarial fever." What is the gold standard for the laboratory diagnosis of this condition?

options.label: Detection of adult worms in the stool
Identification of microfilariae in a thick blood film
Microscopic examination of sputum for eggs
Presence of rhabditiform larvae in the feces
Visualizing trophozoites in a skin biopsy

q10.prompt: A 56-year-old man with a history of consuming raw freshwater fish is diagnosed with a chronic Clonorchis sinensis infection of the biliary tree. This patient is at a significantly increased long-term risk for developing which of the following conditions?
